hero.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Wiki
hero.description: Fight through a dark Edo-period Kyoto as Miyamoto Musashi, mastering parries, Issen strikes, Oni Gauntlet powers, and brutal boss duels against the Genma.

onimushaReleaseDatePlatforms.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Release Date and Platforms
onimushaReleaseDatePlatforms.intro: Use this release snapshot to check launch timing, supported platforms, store coverage, and the current PC hardware targets before you pre-order or wishlist the game.

rows.label: Release Date
rows.details: Capcom confirmed the full launch date for the game.

rows.label: Platforms
rows.value: PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC
rows.details: Console and Windows PC versions are listed across the main official storefronts.

rows.label: PC Stores
rows.value: Steam, Epic Games Store, and Xbox PC
rows.details: PC players can wishlist or pre-order through their preferred store ecosystem.

rows.label: Player Mode
rows.details: The current listings focus on a solo dark fantasy action campaign.

rows.label: Minimum PC Spec
rows.value: Core i5-8400 / Ryzen 3 3100, 16GB RAM, GTX 1660 or RX 5500 XT, 50GB SSD
rows.details: The current Windows requirements target modern hardware with SSD storage.

rows.label: Recommended PC Spec
rows.value: Core i5-10400 / Ryzen 5 3600, 16GB RAM, RTX 2060 Super or RX 6600, 50GB SSD
rows.details: Recommended hardware is aimed at a smoother combat presentation during large encounters.

onimushaDemoDownloadRewards.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Demo Download and Rewards
onimushaDemoDownloadRewards.intro: The free demo is the fastest way to test the pace of combat, the feel of Musashi's defense timing, and the first reward track tied to save data.

cards.title: Where to Download
cards.description: The demo is available through PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, Epic Games Store, and the Steam store page.

cards.title: What You Play
cards.description: The demo runs for about 30 minutes and includes the Kiyomizu-dera route, early combat encounters, and the Sasaki Ganryu boss fight.

cards.title: Reward for Save Data
cards.description: Demo save data unlocks Charm: Kubi Akari in the full release.

cards.title: Carryover Rule
cards.description: Your progress does not transfer into the full game, but the save file still matters for the charm reward.

onimushaPreOrderBonusesEditions.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Pre-Order Bonuses and Editions
onimushaPreOrderBonusesEditions.intro: Choose between the Standard, Deluxe, and Premium Deluxe options based on whether you want gameplay charms only or the broader cosmetic bundle that expands Musashi and ally customization.

editions.name: Standard Edition
editions.summary: Base game plus the core pre-order reward pack.

editions.name: Deluxe Edition
editions.summary: Adds the Deluxe Kit with extra charms, a Musashi outfit, Oni Gauntlet styling, and multiple sword appearances.
includes: Deluxe Kit cosmetics and bonus charms

editions.name: Premium Deluxe Edition
editions.summary: Expands the cosmetic set further with additional haori looks, ally outfits, and the mini digital soundtrack.

onimushaTrailersGameplayVideos.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Trailers and Gameplay Videos
onimushaTrailersGameplayVideos.intro: Use this video lineup to follow the public reveal path from announcement to release-date confirmation without sorting through fan reposts.

videos.title: Release Date Reveal Trailer
videos.summary: Confirms the September 25, 2026 launch date and shows the current pre-release combat presentation.

videos.title: Demo Trailer
videos.summary: Highlights the demo route, Oni Armament attacks, and the tempo of the early encounters.

videos.title: Overview Trailer
videos.summary: Frames Miyamoto Musashi, the dark Kyoto setting, and the tone of the Genma threat.

videos.title: Announcement Trailer
videos.summary: Shows the first major reveal of the new Onimusha entry and its dark fantasy direction.

onimushaBeginnerGuide.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Beginner Walkthrough Guide
onimushaBeginnerGuide.intro: The opening loop is built around measured defense, quick spacing reads, and controlled resource use. Start with these habits if you want the demo route to feel clean instead of chaotic.

steps.title: Read the enemy rhythm before you swing
steps.description: Early encounters reward patience. Let the enemy commit first, then answer with a parry or deflect instead of opening every exchange with offense.

steps.title: Treat Issen as a punish, not a panic button
steps.description: Landing Issen after a clean read gives Musashi his sharpest momentum swing. Practice the timing on smaller foes before the boss sequence.

steps.title: Use souls to keep pressure moving
steps.description: Soul absorption helps you sustain tempo after a clean exchange, so do not leave pickups behind when the arena gives you a safe moment.

steps.title: Enter the Ganryu fight with your route learned
steps.description: The demo becomes much easier once your movement through Kiyomizu-dera is deliberate and you are no longer wasting resources on repeated backtracking.

quickTips: Keep the camera settled before narrow walkway fights.
Deflecting safely is better than forcing every opening into a long combo.
Use short resets between encounters instead of sprinting blindly into the next pack.
The demo is short enough that repeated clean runs are the best practice method.

onimushaCombatOniGauntlet.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Combat and Oni Gauntlet Guide
onimushaCombatOniGauntlet.intro: Onimusha combat stands out when sword timing and Oni power feed each other. The gauntlet is not just a visual prop; it changes how aggressively you can convert defense into damage.

mechanics.name: Parry
mechanics.description: Parry is the direct answer to committed melee swings and sets up strong punish windows.

mechanics.name: Deflect
mechanics.description: Deflect keeps Musashi safe while preserving pressure against faster strings.

mechanics.name: Issen
mechanics.description: Issen is the signature high-impact response when your timing is exact.

mechanics.name: Oni Armament
mechanics.description: Oni Armament expands your offensive reach and helps break the flow of aggressive enemy patterns.

mechanics.name: Soul Absorption
mechanics.description: Souls fuel the rhythm of combat and reward clean finishes during crowded fights.

mechanics.name: Positioning
mechanics.description: Spacing is critical because many encounters hinge on whether you force enemies into narrow approach angles.

combatLoop: Deflect or parry the opening hit.
Convert into Issen or a short sword string.
Absorb souls and reset your spacing before the next wave.

onimushaCharactersStory.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Characters and Story Guide
onimushaCharactersStory.intro: The current story setup centers on Musashi's path through a corrupted Kyoto, the pressure of the Genma invasion, and rival figures who turn each battle into a personal contest.

characters.name: Miyamoto Musashi
characters.description: Musashi leads the campaign with a sword style built around measured defense, punishing counters, and Oni-powered escalation.

characters.name: Sasaki Ganryu
characters.description: Ganryu anchors one of the demo's standout fights and gives players an early read on how intense rival encounters can become.

characters.name: The Oni Gauntlet
characters.description: The gauntlet drives Musashi's supernatural edge and links combat flow to the wider conflict around Genma corruption.

characters.name: Kyoto Under Malice
characters.description: The city is not just a backdrop. Its twisted atmosphere sets the pace for the game's darker encounters and story beats.

onimushaBossesGenmaLocations.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Bosses, Genma, and Locations
onimushaBossesGenmaLocations.intro: The early public material points to a compact set of named threats and locations that define the current pre-launch identity of the game.

entries.name: Sasaki Ganryu
entries.description: A major duel in the demo that tests timing discipline more than raw aggression.

entries.name: Shuten Doji
entries.description: One of the headline monsters tied to the game's darker mythic scale.

entries.name: Genma
entries.description: The Genma presence defines the corruption spreading through Kyoto and gives the world its oppressive tone.

entries.name: Kiyomizu-dera
entries.description: The temple route frames the demo's most immediate navigation and combat lessons.

entries.name: Mount Oe
entries.description: Mount Oe is one of the most visually striking confirmed locations tied to larger-scale danger.

entries.name: Early Kyoto Streets
entries.description: Tight lanes and cursed atmosphere make city encounters feel more dangerous than wide-open arenas.

faq.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about the release, demo, combat structure, and platform support for Onimusha: Way of the Sword.

questions.question: When does Onimusha: Way of the Sword release?
questions.answer: Onimusha: Way of the Sword launches on September 25, 2026.

questions.question: Where can I play Onimusha: Way of the Sword?
questions.answer: Onimusha: Way of the Sword is confirmed for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Windows PC.

questions.question: Does Onimusha: Way of the Sword have a demo?
questions.answer: Yes. A playable demo is available now and includes early combat, the Kiyomizu-dera route, and the Sasaki Ganryu fight.

questions.question: Does Onimusha: Way of the Sword demo carry progress into launch?
questions.answer: Demo progress does not transfer into the full release, but demo save data can unlock Charm: Kubi Akari.

questions.question: Is Onimusha: Way of the Sword single-player only?
questions.answer: The current official listings present Onimusha: Way of the Sword as a single-player swordplay action game.

questions.question: What makes Onimusha: Way of the Sword combat stand out?
questions.answer: The combat focus is built around parry, deflect, Issen timing, soul absorption, and the Oni Gauntlet's powered techniques.
